dialogos.texto_en: (Who's over there%p-2;――%p;)
Ichika, Miku.
Hey, Futaro-kun.
F-Futaro...
Did you find anything interesting?
No, I was just thinking about where to go next.\nDid you guys find any interesting exhibits?
I'm not sure if it was anything interesting,\nbut I enjoyed looking at the model ships earlier.
Model ships?
We were checking out the area\nwith the history on the Ryukyu Kingdom.
In old times, they sailed across the sea to China\nto deliver tribute to the Emperor.
You're acting like you just learned that,\nbut didn't we study it in class?
Well, c'mon, let's just forget that we did for a second.
*sigh*
There was a model of the ship that carried the tribute.\nIt was a shrunken version of the real thing,\nand it looked kinda cute.
Oh, you mean the 'shinkousen.'
There were different colored and shaped flags\nhanging all over the ship.\nThey were said to bring luck.
Apparently, it was used to ensure one's safe journey.
Superstition, I guess.\nBack then, sailing across the sea\nwas dependent on the gods too.
But the flag with the centipede looked a bit scary.\nIt was long, and dangly.
A centipede? Is that also a good luck charm?
Weeell I think there was an explanation\nabout how there was a god who caused storms...
and centipedes were something the gods didn't like.
I see. That's why they hung the centipede flag.
It's guess it's like a charm, huh. Solely relying on the gods\nto cross the sea, not knowing whether you can make it or not.\nPeople in the past must have had a hard time.
I wouldn't want to ride on that ship.\nIt would mean that everyday, I would have to spend my days\npraying for a storm not to come.
I wouldn't have liked that either.\nI feel like I wouldn't be able to handle it mentally.
If I could handle it on my own,\nit would be a different story,\nbut I don't want to leave it to luck.
...That's so you, Futaro.
You think so?
Yeah.
The history of the Ryukyu Kingdom\nwas full of things I didn't know,\nand it was really interesting.
Ohh! So you're interested in history as well as\nJapanese military commanders now? That's great!
There were pirates and stuff as well.\nWeather wasn't the only obstacle in their way.
You mean they'll attack you?
Ships were full of tribute and trade goods,\nwhich were a treasure trove for pirates.
Rather, humans were probably the most troublesome.
Yeah, there was also an explanation for that.\nThey even carried weapons\nin case they were attacked by pirates.
As expected of Futaro-kun.
The history of the Ryukyu Kingdom\nwas full of things I didn't know,\nand it was really interesting.
Ohh! So you're interested in history as well as\nJapanese military commanders now? That's great!
What they got in return was that good, huh.
What they got in return?
By sending tributes to China,\nthey could show their goodwill\nand avoid being invaded from the mainland, right?
I see, making dangerous voyages\nis for the sake of you and everyone else's safety.
Also, going to another country\nmeans you can trade with them.
After all, it was an era where we had to\nrely on divine intervention just to cross the sea.\nCountries that had resources to trade naturally made a profit.
That's right, trade.\nIn Japan, the Shimazu clan in Kyushu\nacted as the window for trade.
Ohhh, your engine's up and running, Miku.
During that time in Japan, Chinese silk was a luxury item.\nThe Shimazu clan was able to make a huge profit\nby buying Chinese silk through the Ryukyu Kingdom.
Oh, silk? I didn't even know that.
To even be able to impress Futaro-kun.\nMiku, you know your stuff.
Teehee...
The history of the Ryukyu Kingdom\nwas full of things I didn't know,\nand it was really interesting.
Ohh! So you're interested in history as well as\nJapanese military commanders now? That's great!
That's a different story.
(Why's that?!?!)
Hey, since we've joined up,\nwhy don't we all look around together,\nincluding Futaro-kun?
S-sure, I don't mind, but...
Is that okay with you too, Miku?\nOr maybe you prefer to be alone with him?
Huh?!\nWh-what do you mean, alone with Futaro...?
I-I have something I want to see,\nso I'll go on ahead.
Huh, Miku?!
Yup, she's avoiding you, that's for sure.
Yeah, there's actually something\nI wanted to ask you about that...
For now, go after Miku.\nIt would be counterproductive for me to go as well, right?
Got it.\nSee you later, Futaro-kun.
